0,35,FALSE,FALSE,10,17,1,25,FALSE,0,0,84,FALSE,FALSE,81.74085575,1.742272965,Index: 35; Auto: False; Heating: False; Concentration: 10.0; Batch: 17; Sample: 1; Heating Block Temperature: 25; Mixed: False; Rest Time (days): 0; Coupon-to-Bath Wait Time (min): 0.0; Relative Humidity: 84; Nitrogen (Side from drop): False; Nitrogen (After blade): False,"# Experimental Report – Membrane Synthesis via NIPS
## Experiment Index: 35
### Overview

This experiment involved the manual fabrication of a polysulfone membrane via non-solvent-induced phase separation (NIPS). The synthesis was carried out under ambient conditions without any automated robotic assistance.
### Solution Preparation

The polymer solution was prepared at a **polysulfone concentration of 10.0 wt%** in PolarClean solvent. The solution belonged to **Batch 17, Sample 1** and was prepared **manually without robotic mixing**. No heating was applied during the mixing process, and the heating block was maintained at ambient temperature (**25°C**). The solution was used **immediately after preparation**, with no resting period (0 days).
### Membrane Casting

The polymer solution was blade-cast onto a coupon and **immediately immersed** into the NIPS bath, with no waiting time between casting and bath immersion (**0 minutes**). No dry nitrogen flow was applied either from the side during drop-casting or after blade casting.
### Environmental Conditions

The casting was performed under notably **high relative humidity conditions (84% RH)**, with no humidity-control measures (e.g., nitrogen purging) employed to counteract potential moisture exposure during the casting process.

This experiment describes the automated fabrication of a polysulfone (PSf) membrane via non-solvent-induced phase separation (NIPS) using a robotic synthesis platform.
## Solution Preparation

A polysulfone solution at a concentration of **15.0 wt%** in PolarClean solvent was used, drawn from **Batch 32 (Sample 1)**. The solution was **premixed manually** prior to use and required **no heating** during the mixing process; consequently, the heating block was maintained at ambient temperature (**25°C**). The solution was used **immediately after preparation**, with no resting period (rest time = **0 days**).
## Membrane Casting

The experiment was conducted under the **automated robotic system**. Following blade casting onto the coupon, the film was **immediately immersed** into the non-solvent (water) bath, with no intentional wait time (**0 minutes**). No humidity control measures were employed; specifically, **no dry nitrogen purge** was applied either during drop-casting or after blade casting. The ambient **relative humidity at the time of synthesis was 46%**.
